questions.prompt: User deleted 'VIP contact: Sarah' from their assistant memory. Within days, the assistant re-inferred and re-added Sarah as important. User complains. Fix?

choices.text: Tell the user the assistant is correct.

Add an explicit block list for previously-deleted entries — they stay deleted unless the user explicitly re-adds. Combine with inference dampening: facts the user removed get re-inferred only on strong signal AND with user confirmation. Otherwise the assistant feels stuck on facts the user didn't want.

questions.explanation: Shadow-profile problems happen when deletion isn't sticky. Block lists + transparent recurrence (ask before re-adding) keep user intent honored. The assistant should respect 'I removed this' — not infer it back on the next interaction.

questions.prompt: Why build deletion plumbing early in an assistant product?

choices.text: Regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA) requires actual deletion within fixed timeframes — including derived data like embeddings and summaries. Retrofitting deletion under regulatory pressure is painful; building it as a first-class capability up front is much cheaper. Also: incident response sometimes needs fast removal of specific memory items.

questions.explanation: Real deletion — including derived data — is hard to retrofit. GDPR/CCPA require it; users will eventually request it; incident response sometimes needs urgent purges. Bake it into the storage layer from v1, document the process, and have a fast-track flow for urgent requests.
